title: Smoky Fluffy Spanish Chicken Paella
description: A low‑sodium, smoky paella that combines tender chicken, fragrant saffron rice, and a creamy tahini drizzle for a date‑night twist.

ingredients.name: Saffron threads, steeped in warm water

instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large paella pan over medium heat. Add the chicken thighs skin‑side down and sear until golden, about 4 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Add the remaining tbsp of olive oil to the pan, then sauté onion, garlic, and red bell pepper until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in smoked paprika and the saffron‑infused water, cooking for 1 minute to release the flavors.
Add the rice, coating it well with the oil and spices. Stir for 2 minutes.
Pour in the white wine and let it reduce until almost evaporated, about 3 minutes.
Return the chicken to the pan, nestling it into the rice. Add the low‑sodium broth and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover loosely, and simmer for 15 minutes.
Stir in the frozen peas and let the paella finish cooking until the rice is al dente and the liquid is absorbed, about 5 more minutes. Remove the lid.
In a small bowl, whisk tahini with lime juice, a splash of the paella liquid, and a pinch of salt to create a creamy sauce.
Drizzle the tahini sauce over the paella, sprinkle chopped parsley, and serve with lemon wedges for squeezing.
Enjoy a smoky, fluffy, date‑night paella that’s both low‑sodium and irresistibly comforting.
